title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
View this email in your browser

The Brew Shop Weekly Update

Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

SHOP THIS WEEK’S NEWSLETTER HERE
SHOP OUR ENTIRE STOCK OF BEER, WINE & HOMEBREW HERE
 
Spring Wine Fest!

NEXT THURSDAY! Join us for The Brew Shop's Annual Spring Wine Festival on Thursday, May 14 from 5:30 - 8:30 pm!

- Walk-about tasting featuring 20 different Spring-forward wines

- Outdoors and indoors! Enjoy the Spring weather with outdoor tasting stations along our front sidewalk

- Featuring artisanal cheese and crackers

- Discounts for retail purchases on all wines

- Chat, learn and sip wine with our wonderful team of in-the-know wine distributors

- A great way to stock up for Spring outings, family gatherings and patio time

To give us a little more space, the tasting has been split into two sessions: 5:30-7 pm and 7-8:30 pm. Tickets are $20 per person ($15 for Wine Club Members). Please choose the time slot that works best for you!

Wine Club Member? Remember to put in your special code at check out for 10% off!

PURCHASE SPRING WINE FEST TICKETS HERE
 
Friday Free Tasting!

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Silver Branch for our free tasting! Nik will be on hand pouring a lovely spread of styles from one of our favorite Maryland breweries. Scroll down to see our tasting line-up for May.

Current Hot Crowler/Growler Fills

Firestone Walker Pfreal West Coast IPA

RAR / Hop Butcher Thunderstorms Inside Hazy DIPA

 
New wines in the door - Part 1:

Mary Taylor Muscadet Blanc de Blancs: As importer Mary Taylor says, we're calling this Blanc de Blancs “Muscadet” with quotes because while this is certainly a sparkling wine from Muscadet it doesn't fit into the required, strict rules of either AOP Muscadet or AOP Crémant de Loire. Rules are meant to be bent and we're smitten on this sparkler with delicate melon, richness and a round creamy texture. Organic.

Georgiev/Milkov Pet Nat Funky Mavrud: After making wine across the globe, two friends and fellow oenology graduates, Petar Georgiev and Radostin Milkov, returned home to champion Bulgaria's own ancient indigenous varieties. Their crowning achievement is this utterly fun wine: Bulgaria's first-ever pét-nat made from Mavrud. Surprisingly not-so-funky with raspberries, strawberries, and cranberries alongside citrus and mineral undertones. Native Yeast, Hand Harvested & No Added Sulfites.

Bononia Estate Gomotartzi Dimyat Vermentino: An easy-going and crisp white blend from northern Bulgaria that makes me want to learn more about Bulgarian wine — and wonder why it's taken me this long to discover it. Check out Beth’s wine write-up below for all the details!

Domaine de Sulauze Galinette Blanc: Galinette first made its way into the store for a personal taste test (yes, that happens a lot!). And, oh my, it's gorgeous. Vinified in a combination of concrete tanks and old oak foudres, Galinette is both effortless and complex with citrus, white flowers, and a lovely streak of sea-salt minerality. Biodynamic, Native Yeast & Unfiltered.

Fableist Chardonnay: This Central Coast Chardonnay from the imaginative and shop favorite Andrew Jones is anything but a one-trick pony — 60% is fermented in barrel with daily bâtonnage for richness, while the rest goes into stainless steel to preserve freshness and bright acidity. Warm and balanced with pear, apple blossom and a bright, lively finish. Native Yeast.

New wines in the door - Part 2:

Liquid Geography Rose: While France gets all the attention, Spain has been quietly crafting some of the most characterful and downright delicious rosés in the world. Made from 100% Mencía from 60-year-old vines in Spain's Bierzo, Liquid Geography is vibrant and fresh with strawberry, red currant, white pepper and a mineral finish. Even better, 100% of Liquid Geography's profits are donated to causes including cancer research and children's education. Organic & Vegan.

Bononia Estate Gomotartzi Gamza and Cabernet Franc: Located along the Danube River in northwest Bulgaria, Bononia is housed in a building that dates back to 1895 — originally one of the first breweries in the Balkans, now restored into a modern winery. Natalyia, our distributor and a Bulgarian native, poured this Bulgarian blend with a slight chill... and I'm obsessed. The star of this bottle is Gamza — an ancient, thin-skinned indigenous grape — blended with Cabernet Franc. The result is fresh and juicy with pomegranate, cranberry, tart cherry and savory dried herbs.

Early Mountain Foothills Red: Nestled in the foothills of the Blue Ridge Mountains, it's easy to say we're fangirls of Early Mountain's winemaker Maya Hood White. Just ask Julie about that time I neglected to introduce her when Maya popped by the store unannounced... now we're in a fight. Maya's talents shine in this Bordeaux-inspired Virginia blend which is remarkably easy-going and lively with red and dark fruits, a hint of dried herbs like tarragon, a touch of graphite, soft tannins, and bright acidity.

Bodega de Edgar El CabRhone: Born in Mexico and raised in California, Edgar got his start in the restaurant industry then worked his way up as a winemaking apprentice in Paso Robles to respected boutique winemaker and owner. In this French-inspired blend, Edgar combines Bordeaux Cabernets and Rhône varietals that could never legally share a bottle in their homeland, but magically synergize for a New World interpretation. Bold and enchanting wine with notes of cassis, dark blueberries, coffee, chocolate, and red fruits.

Wine we’re drinking this week:

A couple of weekends ago, Mollie - one of our much loved and long-tenured Brew Crew members - went to a wine festival in St. Michaels where she tasted and fell in love with several Bulgarian wines. For the good of the store (or herself, wink), Mollie told the distributor, Nataliya, to visit us at The Brew Shop to see if we'd be interested in Bulgarian wines. Mollie was right; I, too, am obsessed! Three new Bulgarian wines hit the shelf this week, including the easy-going and crisp white blend Bononia Estate Gomotartzi Dimyat & Vermentino.

Located along the Danube River in northwest Bulgaria, Bononia is owned by the Yotov family, whose day job is making parts for the aviation industry - hence the logo, which is a propeller. Apparently, when you're done making components for aircraft, the logical next step is... fine wine. The winery is housed in a building that dates back to 1895 and was originally one of the first breweries in the Balkans, now restored into a modern winery. The Yotov family began planting and opened the winery in 2013, and have since acquired 160 hectares under vine.

Pour yourself a glass of Gomotartzi and prepare to be charmed. Gomotartzi blends the native Dimyat grape with the more familiar Vermentino. Coming from the cool-climate region near the Danube River along the border of Romania, Gomotartzi is wonderfully precise and crisp. Aromas of flowers and fresh grass fill the glass. Made entirely unoaked, the palate is lively and mineral-driven with citrus zest, green apple and apricot. In true Mollie-speak, Gomotartzi Dimyat & Vermentino is the wine equivalent of discovering an addictive hidden track at the end of a Taylor Swift album (such as my personal pick, Hits Different from Midnights) - completely unexpected, quietly thrilling, and excellent on repeat.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Beth.

SHOP OUR ENTIRE WINE SELECTION HERE
 

May Tasting Events are spring ready!

 

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
